verdict_reason: The single disagreement entry on the doublet 'kaṁ ghātayati hanti kam' is doctrinally substantive. It correctly names that both modern translations preserve the surface doublet (kill anyone / cause anyone to be killed) but flatten the interpretive scope. Shankara's reading scopes the doublet to denying both direct and indirect agency to the man-of-realization, generalised to all action. Ramanuja's reading scopes the doublet to denying that the realised one 'causes the death' of any embodied self (gods, animals, immovables), specifically as setup for 2.22's clothes simile. The explanation captures three distinct facts: (a) the philological-translation pattern (modern translators flatten), (b) the Advaita stake (general renunciation argument), (c) the Vishishtadvaita stake (sets up 2.22's affect-reframing). One tight paragraph carrying all three. Score 9, PASS.
